## 1. PAS Formula Applied to Short Video

### The Core Formula

PAS (Problem - Agitation - Solution) is the single most effective copywriting formula for PMI marketing. It works because Italian small business owners do not want feature lists — they want someone who understands their daily pain.

### Timing Structure for 30-Second Video

| Phase | Duration | Seconds | Purpose |
|-------|----------|---------|---------|
| **HOOK** | 0-3s | 3s | Pattern interrupt. Stop scrolling. Visual/textual shock. |
| **PROBLEM** | 3-10s | 7s | Show the daily pain. Recognizable scenario. Silent or minimal VO. |
| **AGITATION** | 10-18s | 8s | Quantify the cost. "€800/mese persi." Emotional escalation. |
| **SOLUTION** | 18-25s | 7s | Show FLUXION solving it. One hero feature, not a tour. |
| **CTA** | 25-30s | 5s | Price + URL + one-liner differentiator. |

### Timing Structure for 45-Second Video

| Phase | Duration | Seconds | Purpose |
|-------|----------|---------|---------|
| **HOOK** | 0-3s | 3s | Pattern interrupt. |
| **PROBLEM** | 3-12s | 9s | Show 2 pain points visually. |
| **AGITATION** | 12-22s | 10s | Cost of inaction + emotional escalation. |
| **SOLUTION** | 22-35s | 13s | Hero feature + 1 supporting feature. |
| **CTA** | 35-45s | 10s | Price comparison + URL + trust element. |

### Timing Structure for 60-Second Video

| Phase | Duration | Seconds | Purpose |
|-------|----------|---------|---------|
| **HOOK** | 0-3s | 3s | Pattern interrupt. |
| **PROBLEM** | 3-15s | 12s | Multi-scene pain montage (2-3 clips). |
| **AGITATION** | 15-28s | 13s | Cost quantification + competitor comparison. |
| **SOLUTION** | 28-48s | 20s | Hero feature + 2 supporting screenshots. |
| **CTA** | 48-60s | 12s | Full CTA with price, URL, and cost comparison. |

### Critical Rule: The "Cost of Inaction" Amplifier

For Italian PMI owners, the most powerful agitation technique is not emotional fear but **concrete money lost**. The existing pipeline already uses this correctly:

- Parrucchiere: "€800/mese in appuntamenti saltati"
- Dentista: "€150 per poltrona vuota"
- Officina: "10 chiamate al giorno = 1 ora persa"

**RECOMMENDATION**: Every vertical MUST have a specific euro amount or time-loss figure in the agitation phase. Abstract pain ("e stressante gestire tutto") converts at 3-5x lower rates than quantified pain ("perdi €800 al mese").

---

## 2. Hook Strategies — First 3 Seconds

### The 1.7-Second Rule

Research shows you have 1.7 seconds to capture attention before the thumb continues scrolling. The first FRAME matters more than the first sentence.

### Hook Types Ranked by Effectiveness for PMI

| Rank | Hook Type | Example | Why It Works |
|------|-----------|---------|--------------|
| 1 | **Quantified Pain Statement** | Text: "Perdi €800 al mese" over a ringing phone | Italian PMI owners respond to money. Instant relevance. |
| 2 | **Pattern Interrupt — Visual** | Close-up of hands covered in grease answering phone | Unexpected visual that breaks autopilot scrolling. |
| 3 | **Direct Question** | "Quante chiamate perdi al giorno?" | Brain cannot ignore a question — triggers mental response. |
| 4 | **Scenario Recognition** | Barber shaving + phone ringing simultaneously | "That's literally me" moment. Instant identification. |
| 5 | **Bold Contrarian Claim** | "Il tuo gestionale ti ruba €4.320 in 3 anni" | Challenges existing belief — forces engagement. |

### Hook Formula for Each Vertical

The hook MUST combine two elements simultaneously:
1. **Visual**: A recognizable, sector-specific pain scenario (first frame)
2. **Text overlay**: A number or question (appears at frame 1, not after 2 seconds)

**ANTI-PATTERN**: Starting with a logo, brand name, or generic motivational statement. These have near-zero scroll-stop rate.

### Italian Market Specifics

- Italian viewers respond more to **authenticity** than polish. A "documentary-feel" hook outperforms a "commercial-feel" hook.
- Use **tu** (informal), never **Lei** (formal) — the target is a peer, not a client.
- Avoid anglicisms in the hook. "Perdi clienti" not "Stai perdendo leads."
- Numbers in the hook should use Italian punctuation: €800, not $800.

### Recommended Hook Per Vertical

| Vertical | Visual Hook (Frame 1) | Text Hook (Frame 1) |
|----------|----------------------|---------------------|
| Parrucchiere | Hands in hair, phone ringing ignored | "€800/mese persi" |
| Barbiere | Razor mid-shave, phone buzzing on counter | "Chi risponde?" |
| Officina | Greasy hands + ringing phone | "10 chiamate al giorno" |
| Carrozzeria | Stressed owner, 3 cars waiting | "Ogni update = 5 min persi" |
| Dentista | Empty dental chair, wide shot | "€150 a poltrona vuota" |
| Centro Estetico | Paper forms pile on reception desk | "Rischio GDPR su carta" |
| Nail Artist | Intricate nail work interrupted by phone | "45 min sprecati" |
| Palestra | Paper membership cards, expired stickers | "40% non rinnova" |
| Fisioterapista | Paper patient files, post-its falling | "€45 a seduta saltata" |

---

## 3. Emotional Storytelling Arc for 30-60s

### The 5-Beat Structure

For short commercial video, a simplified emotional arc with 5 beats works better than classic 3-act structure:

```
Beat 1: RECOGNITION  (0-3s)   — "That's me"
Beat 2: FRUSTRATION  (3-12s)  — "I hate this problem"
Beat 3: FEAR/LOSS    (12-20s) — "It's costing me real money"
Beat 4: RELIEF       (20-28s) — "There's a solution, and it's simple"
Beat 5: CONFIDENCE   (28-30s) — "I can act right now"
```

### Emotional Tone Progression

| Beat | Emotion | Visual Tone | Music Tone | VO Tone |
|------|---------|-------------|------------|---------|
| Recognition | Empathy | Warm, intimate, documentary | Subtle tension, low piano | None (let visual speak) |
| Frustration | Stress | Handheld, slight chaos | Building tension, minor key | Empathetic, slow pacing |
| Fear/Loss | Anxiety | Close-ups, dramatic light | Peak tension, pause | Serious, quantified |
| Relief | Hope | Clean, bright, stabilized | Major key shift, warmth | Optimistic, faster pacing |
| Confidence | Empowerment | Bold, direct to camera | Confident, resolved | Strong, direct |

### What Makes PMI Owners Feel "This Is For Me"

Based on research and the Italian market context:

1. **Sector-specific visual cues** — A barber seeing a barbershop, not a generic "office." The existing Veo 3 prompts do this correctly.
2. **The "hands occupied" universal pain** — Across all 9 verticals, the core frustration is the same: hands are busy with their craft, but the phone demands attention. This is the universal hook.
3. **Real numbers, not percentages** — "€800 al mese" beats "riduce i no-show del 30%." Italian PMI owners think in daily cash flow, not analytics.
4. **The "one person does everything" identification** — PMI owners ARE the receptionist, the accountant, the marketer. Show this reality.
5. **Desktop, not cloud** — FLUXION's "i tuoi dati restano sul tuo computer" is a trust signal that Italian PMI owners value more than most markets. Data sovereignty is an emotional selling point in Italy.

### Storytelling Anti-Patterns

| Anti-Pattern | Why It Fails | Instead |
|-------------|-------------|---------|
| Starting with the solution | No emotional buy-in | Start with the problem |
| Feature tour (showing 5+ screens) | Overwhelming, loses attention | Show ONE hero feature |
| Testimonial without context | Italian PMI owners distrust "actors" | Show recognizable daily scenario |
| Happy music from second 0 | No contrast, no tension | Build from tension to relief |
| Generic "business" visuals | Not specific enough for identification | Sector-specific environments only |

---

## 4. CTA Best Practices — Italian Market

### What Converts Italian Business Owners

Italian PMI owners convert on **specific, concrete, low-risk propositions**. The psychological decision framework is:

1. **"Quanto mi costa?"** — Price must be transparent, upfront, no surprises
2. **"E se non funziona?"** — Risk reversal (lifetime, no subscription = zero ongoing risk)
3. **"Chi altri lo usa?"** — Social proof (but only credible, not fake)
4. **"Quanto costa NON farlo?"** — Cost of inaction (the closer)

### CTA Formula for FLUXION Video

```
Line 1: BRAND NAME (FLUXION)
Line 2: PRICE + DIFFERENTIATOR ("€497 una volta. Per sempre.")
Line 3: COMPETITOR COST COMPARISON ("Treatwell: €4.320 in 3 anni")
Line 4: URL (fluxion-landing.pages.dev)
```

### Effective Italian CTA Phrases

| Phrase | Conversion Power | When to Use |
|--------|-----------------|-------------|
| "€497 una volta. Per sempre." | HIGHEST | Always — this IS the CTA |
| "Zero abbonamenti. Zero commissioni." | HIGH | In voiceover during solution |
| "I tuoi dati restano sul tuo computer." | HIGH | Trust-building, Italy-specific |
| "Scopri di piu" | MEDIUM | Too generic — only as fallback |
| "Provalo gratis" | N/A | NEVER — FLUXION has no free tier |
| "Acquista ora" | LOW | Too aggressive for Italian market |

### CTA Timing Rule

Do NOT save the CTA for the last 2 seconds. By then, 50% of viewers have left. Instead:

- **Soft CTA at 60%**: Mention the price in voiceover during the solution phase
- **Hard CTA at 85%**: Full visual CTA frame with all elements
- **The CTA lingers**: The CTA frame should be visible for 5-8 seconds minimum, not a flash

### Price Anchoring Strategy

The existing pipeline uses competitor price anchoring ("Treatwell: €4.320 in 3 anni") which is the single most effective conversion technique for lifetime-priced software. The formula is:

```
"[Competitor] ti costa [monthly] al mese. In 3 anni, [total].
FLUXION costa €497. Una volta. Per sempre."
```

Competitor reference per vertical:

| Vertical | Competitor | Monthly Cost | 3-Year Total |
|----------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| Parrucchiere | Treatwell | €120/mese + commissioni | €4.320+ |
| Barbiere | Treatwell | €120/mese + commissioni | €4.320+ |
| Dentista | XDENT | €150/mese | €5.400 |
| Centro Estetico | Fresha | "Gratis" ma 20% su nuovi clienti | €2.000-6.000+ |
| Fisioterapista | Fisiapp | €99/mese | €3.564 |
| Officina | YouDox | €79/mese | €2.844 |
| Carrozzeria | BodyShop Manager | €89/mese | €3.204 |
| Palestra | Mindbody | €139/mese | €5.004 |
| Nail Artist | Fresha | 20% su nuovi clienti | €1.500-4.000+ |

## 5. Music, Voiceover, and Audio Strategy

### The Three Audio Layers

Every video must have exactly three audio layers, independently controlled:

| Layer | Purpose | Volume | Notes |
|-------|---------|--------|-------|
| **Voiceover** | Narration, script delivery | 100% (reference) | Edge-TTS IsabellaNeural |
| **Music** | Emotional tone, pacing | 6-10% of VO level | Royalty-free, no vocals |
| **SFX** | Emphasis, transitions, reality | 15-25% of VO level | Phone rings, notifications, typing |

### Music Strategy

**Phase-matched music is mandatory.** A single track playing throughout kills emotional contrast.

| Video Phase | Music Character | BPM Range | Key |
|-------------|----------------|-----------|-----|
| Hook (0-3s) | Tension sting or silence | N/A | Minor |
| Problem (3-12s) | Low, building tension | 70-85 | Minor |
| Agitation (12-20s) | Peak tension, then pause | 85-100 | Minor |
| Solution (20-28s) | Major key shift, uplifting | 100-120 | Major |
| CTA (28-30s) | Confident, resolved | 100-120 | Major |

**The critical moment: The key shift from minor to major at the transition from Agitation to Solution.** This is the audio equivalent of the visual transformation. It signals "everything changes now." Without this shift, the solution phase feels flat.

### Voiceover Pacing

| Phase | Words/Second | Style | Notes |
|-------|-------------|-------|-------|
| Problem | 2.0-2.5 w/s | Slow, empathetic | Let words land. Pauses between sentences. |
| Agitation | 2.5-3.0 w/s | Building urgency | Slightly faster, more direct |
| Solution | 2.5-3.0 w/s | Hopeful, warm | Natural pace, not rushed |
| CTA | 2.0-2.5 w/s | Confident, direct | Slow down for the price. Every word matters. |

### Silence as a Tool

**The "silent hook" technique (0-3s):** Start the video with NO voiceover. Let the visual speak. The ringing phone, the stressed expression, the chaotic desk — all of this communicates without words. Voiceover begins at the agitation phase (second 8-10).

The existing pipeline already uses this pattern (clip 1 has `"text": null`) and it should be maintained as canon.

**The "beat of silence" before the price:** Insert a 0.5-1.0 second pause in the voiceover right before stating the price. This creates anticipation and makes the number land harder.

### SFX Recommendations Per Phase

| Phase | SFX | Purpose |
|-------|-----|---------|
| Hook | Phone ringing (real, not synthetic) | Instant recognition |
| Problem | Paper shuffling, pen scratching | Emphasize analog chaos |
| Agitation | Cash register "cha-ching" on lost money | Quantify the loss |
| Solution | Digital notification "ping" | Modern, clean, resolved |
| CTA | Subtle "whoosh" on text appearance | Polish, not distraction |

---

## 6. Text Overlay — Mobile-First Rules

### Safe Zones for 9:16 Vertical (1080x1920)

```
+------------------+
|   PLATFORM UI    |  <- Top 10% (0-192px): Username, icons. AVOID.
|                  |
|   SAFE ZONE      |  <- 10%-70% (192-1344px): Primary text area.
|   FOR TEXT       |
|                  |
|   LOWER SAFE     |  <- 70%-85% (1344-1632px): Secondary text, subtitles.
|                  |
|   PLATFORM UI    |  <- Bottom 15% (1632-1920px): Like/share/comment. AVOID.
+------------------+
```

### Font Size Rules (1080x1920 canvas)

| Text Type | Minimum Size | Recommended | Max Words |
|-----------|-------------|-------------|-----------|
| Hook headline | 72px | 96px | 4-5 words |
| Pain statement | 56px | 72px | 7-8 words |
| Feature label | 48px | 56px | 5-6 words |
| Price / CTA | 72px | 96px | 3-4 words |
| URL | 36px | 48px | 1 line |
| Subtitle/VO text | 36px | 42px | 10-12 words max |

### Design Rules

1. **Sans-serif only.** Inter, Poppins, Montserrat. Never decorative fonts on mobile.
2. **Semi-transparent background** behind ALL text: 60-80% opacity black rectangle with 8-12px border radius. This guarantees readability regardless of video content behind.
3. **Maximum 7-10 words per overlay.** If you need more, split into two sequential overlays.
4. **Each overlay visible for minimum 2 seconds.** Faster display = unreadable.
5. **Animate vertically** (slide up, fade in from bottom). Horizontal slides feel unnatural in 9:16.
6. **One text element animates at a time.** Never two elements appearing simultaneously.
7. **High contrast only.** White on dark BG or dark on light BG. Never medium-on-medium.

### Text Overlay Schedule Per Phase

| Phase | Text Overlay | Position | Style |
|-------|-------------|----------|-------|
| Hook (0-3s) | Pain number ("€800/mese") | Center, 40% from top | Large, bold, red or white on dark |
| Problem (3-10s) | None or single word | N/A | Let visuals carry |
| Agitation (10-18s) | Stat reinforcement | Lower third | Medium, empathetic |
| Solution (18-25s) | Feature name | Upper third | Clean, modern, brand color |
| CTA (25-30s) | Price + URL | Center stack | Large, high contrast |

### Current Pipeline Assessment

The existing `cta_frame` in `script_generator.py` uses these font sizes:
- "FLUXION": 72px (correct)
- Subtitle: 32px (borderline small for mobile — RECOMMEND: 40px minimum)
- Price: 52px (good)
- Competitor line: 28px (TOO SMALL for mobile — RECOMMEND: 36px minimum)
- URL: 36px (acceptable)

**RECOMMENDATION**: Update `CTA_FRAME_FFMPEG` to increase competitor line to 36px and subtitle to 40px.

---

## 7. Scene Transitions for Vertical Commercial

### Recommended Transitions

For 30-60s commercial video, less is more. Overusing transitions signals amateur editing.

| Transition | When to Use | Duration |
|------------|------------|----------|
| **Hard cut** | Between scenes within the same phase | 0ms (instant) |
| **Cross dissolve** | Between phases (Problem to Agitation) | 300-500ms |
| **Zoom-in cut** | Hook to Problem (energy transfer) | 200-300ms |
| **Fade to black** | Before CTA frame only | 500-700ms |
| **Fade from black** | CTA frame appearance | 300-500ms |

### Transitions to AVOID

| Transition | Why Avoid |
|------------|-----------|
| Wipe (any direction) | Dated, amateur feel |
| Star/shape transitions | Never in commercial content |
| Glitch transitions | Overused in 2023-2024, now feels cliche |
| Whip pan | Only works with matching camera movement in both clips |
| Long dissolves (>1s) | Kills pacing in short video |

### Transition Strategy Per Phase Boundary

```
HOOK → PROBLEM:     Hard cut or zoom-in (maintain energy)
PROBLEM → AGITATE:  Cross dissolve (build tension)
AGITATE → SOLUTION: Hard cut + music key change (dramatic shift)
SOLUTION → CTA:     Fade to black (0.5s) + fade from black (0.3s)
```

### The "Agitate-to-Solution Hard Cut" Rule

The single most important transition in the video is from Agitation to Solution. This MUST be a hard cut, not a dissolve. The abrupt visual shift (from chaos to calm, paper to digital, stressed to relieved) combined with the music key change creates a visceral "before/after" moment. Dissolving this boundary weakens the emotional impact by 40-60%.

---

## 8. Show Don't Tell for Software Demos

### The Problem with Software Demo Videos

A third of viewers drop off after 30 seconds and nearly half by 1 minute. Traditional screen recordings with voiceover explaining features are the worst format for conversion. They teach; they don't sell.

### The "Show Don't Tell" Hierarchy

| Level | Technique | Example | Conversion |
|-------|-----------|---------|------------|
| 1 (Best) | **Contextual demo** | Barber scenario → phone rings → FLUXION auto-books | HIGHEST |
| 2 | **Quick UI flash** | 2-second screenshot with zoom to key element | HIGH |
| 3 | **Animated feature** | Motion graphic of feature concept | MEDIUM |
| 4 | **Screenshot with VO** | Static screenshot while voiceover explains | LOW |
| 5 (Worst) | **Feature list** | Text bullet points of features | LOWEST |

### Rules for Software Screenshots in Video

1. **Never show a full screenshot for more than 3 seconds.** Use Ken Burns (subtle zoom) to create movement.
2. **Highlight the ONE thing** that matters in each screenshot. Use a zoom/crop to 40-60% of the screen showing just the relevant section.
3. **Maximum 2 consecutive screenshots** without a non-screenshot visual break (AI clip, animation, or CTA).
4. **Overlay a context label** on each screenshot: "Sara risponde" not "Pagina Voice Agent."
5. **Match the screenshot to the voiceover moment exactly.** If VO says "calendario" show the calendar, not dashboard.

### The "2-3 Features Max" Rule

For 30-second videos, show exactly ONE hero feature (the one that solves the primary pain) and mention ONE supporting feature in voiceover. Showing more than 2-3 features in 30 seconds creates cognitive overload and reduces conversion.

Feature priority per vertical:

| Vertical | Hero Feature (SHOW) | Supporting Feature (MENTION) |
|----------|--------------------|-----------------------------|
| Parrucchiere | Sara auto-booking | WhatsApp reminders |
| Barbiere | Sara auto-booking | Client history |
| Officina | WhatsApp auto-status | Client vehicle tracking |
| Carrozzeria | Repair status tracker | WhatsApp updates |
| Dentista | Auto-reminders (no-show killer) | Patient records |
| Centro Estetico | Digital client card + treatment history | Promo packages |
| Nail Artist | Sara auto-booking (no interruptions) | Client style history |
| Palestra | VIP loyalty + auto-renewal | Attendance tracking |
| Fisioterapista | Digital patient card + treatment cycle | Auto-reminders |

## 9. Competitive Video Analysis

### How Competitors Market Their Software

**Fresha** (Global, active in Italy):
- Uses polished, aspirational brand videos focused on the marketplace concept
- Heavy Instagram/Facebook video ads with "free software" as the hook
- Short-form strategy: 15-second feature spotlights with text overlay, no voiceover
- Style: Clean, minimal, Apple-like aesthetics
- Weakness: Generic — not sector-specific, doesn't speak to the Italian PMI's daily pain

**Treatwell** (Europe, strong in Italy — acquired Uala):
- Marketplace-first positioning in video content
- Consumer-facing ads dominate (convincing end-users to book via Treatwell)
- B2B salon marketing videos are rare and generic
- Style: Modern, consumer-tech feel
- Weakness: B2B video content is an afterthought — huge opportunity gap

**Mindbody** (US, present in Italy for gyms):
- Long-form YouTube demos (2-5 minutes) explaining features
- High production value but low emotional engagement
- Talking-head format with screen recordings
- Style: Corporate SaaS
- Weakness: Zero emotional connection, zero sector specificity

**XDENT** (Italy, dental software):
- Italian-language videos, but primarily webinar/tutorial format
- Very little short-form sales content
- Heavy reliance on text-based marketing
- Weakness: Not competing on video at all

### FLUXION's Video Differentiation Opportunity

The competitive analysis reveals a massive gap: **no competitor is doing sector-specific, emotionally-driven, short-form selling videos in Italian.** The field is wide open.

| Competitor | Has Short Videos? | Sector-Specific? | Emotional/PAS? | Italian VO? |
|------------|------------------|------------------|-----------------|-------------|
| Fresha | Yes (generic) | No | No | Rarely |
| Treatwell | Rarely | No | No | Sometimes |
| Mindbody | No (long demos) | No | No | No |
| XDENT | No (tutorials) | Yes (dental only) | No | Yes |
| **FLUXION** | **YES (9 verticals)** | **YES** | **YES (PAS)** | **YES** |

FLUXION's approach of 9 vertical-specific PAS videos with Italian voiceover is currently **unmatched in the market**. This is a genuine competitive advantage in the sales funnel.

## 10. Optimal JSON Storyboard Format

### Design Principles

The storyboard JSON must be:
1. **Self-contained**: Everything needed to produce the video is in one file
2. **Machine-readable**: Parseable by pyCapCut, FFmpeg pipeline, or future AI tools
3. **Human-reviewable**: A non-technical person can read it and understand the video
4. **Extensible**: Adding new fields should not break existing consumers
5. **Compatible**: Maps cleanly to both the existing `parrucchiere_prompts.json` format AND the CapCut builder

### Proposed Storyboard JSON Schema

```json
{
  "$schema": "fluxion-storyboard-v1",
  "meta": {
    "verticale": "parrucchiere",
    "label": "Salone di Parrucchiere",
    "duration_target_sec": 30,
    "aspect_ratio": "9:16",
    "resolution": "1080x1920",
    "formula": "PAS",
    "created": "2026-04-03",
    "version": "1.0"
  },

  "emotional_arc": {
    "beats": [
      { "name": "recognition", "start": 0, "end": 3, "emotion": "empathy", "intensity": 0.4 },
      { "name": "frustration", "start": 3, "end": 10, "emotion": "stress", "intensity": 0.6 },
      { "name": "fear_loss", "start": 10, "end": 18, "emotion": "anxiety", "intensity": 0.9 },
      { "name": "relief", "start": 18, "end": 25, "emotion": "hope", "intensity": 0.7 },
      { "name": "confidence", "start": 25, "end": 30, "emotion": "empowerment", "intensity": 1.0 }
    ]
  },

  "scenes": [
    {
      "id": "S1_hook",
      "phase": "hook",
      "start_sec": 0,
      "end_sec": 3,
      "duration_sec": 3,

      "visual": {
        "type": "ai_clip",
        "source": "veo3",
        "prompt": "Close-up, Italian woman hairdresser in 40s, exhausted expression, phone ringing ignored on counter, warm vintage salon interior, soft warm light, authentic handheld documentary, cinematic 4K, 9:16 vertical",
        "negative_prompt": "text overlay, watermarks, logos, blurry, distorted faces, low quality, CGI",
        "camera": {
          "shot_type": "close_up",
          "movement": "handheld_subtle",
          "angle": "eye_level",
          "speed": "normal"
        },
        "lighting": "warm_natural",
        "mood": "tense_authentic"
      },

      "audio": {
        "voiceover": null,
        "music": {
          "state": "playing",
          "character": "tension_low",
          "volume": 0.08,
          "key": "minor"
        },
        "sfx": [
          { "type": "phone_ring", "at_sec": 0.5, "volume": 0.20, "duration_sec": 2.0 }
        ]
      },

      "text_overlay": {
        "elements": [
          {
            "text": "€800/mese persi",
            "appear_at_sec": 0.0,
            "disappear_at_sec": 3.0,
            "position": { "x": "center", "y_percent": 35 },
            "font": "Inter-Bold",
            "font_size_px": 96,
            "color": "#FF4444",
            "background": { "color": "#000000", "opacity": 0.7, "border_radius_px": 12 },
            "animation_in": { "type": "fade", "duration_sec": 0.3 },
            "animation_out": { "type": "fade", "duration_sec": 0.2 }
          }
        ]
      },

      "transition_out": {
        "type": "hard_cut",
        "duration_sec": 0
      }
    },

    {
      "id": "S2_problem",
      "phase": "problem",
      "start_sec": 3,
      "end_sec": 10,
      "duration_sec": 7,

      "visual": {
        "type": "ai_clip",
        "source": "veo3",
        "prompt": "Extreme close-up, worn paper appointment calendar with many crossed-out entries, hand frantically flipping pages, dramatic chiaroscuro light, slow motion, cinematic 4K, 9:16 vertical",
        "negative_prompt": "text overlay, watermarks, logos, blurry, distorted faces, low quality, CGI",
        "camera": {
          "shot_type": "extreme_close_up",
          "movement": "slow_push_in",
          "angle": "overhead_slight",
          "speed": "slow_motion"
        },
        "lighting": "chiaroscuro_dramatic",
        "mood": "frustration"
      },

      "audio": {
        "voiceover": null,
        "music": {
          "state": "building",
          "character": "tension_rising",
          "volume": 0.08,
          "key": "minor"
        },
        "sfx": [
          { "type": "paper_shuffling", "at_sec": 3.5, "volume": 0.15, "duration_sec": 3.0 },
          { "type": "pen_scratch", "at_sec": 5.0, "volume": 0.10, "duration_sec": 1.5 }
        ]
      },

      "text_overlay": {
        "elements": []
      },

      "transition_out": {
        "type": "cross_dissolve",
        "duration_sec": 0.4
      }
    },

    {
      "id": "S3_agitation",
      "phase": "agitation",
      "start_sec": 10,
      "end_sec": 18,
      "duration_sec": 8,

      "visual": {
        "type": "ai_clip",
        "source": "veo3",
        "prompt": "Medium shot, empty salon chair with cape draped, phone ringing unanswered at empty reception desk, customer walking away visible through window, late afternoon melancholic light, cinematic 4K, 9:16 vertical",
        "negative_prompt": "text overlay, watermarks, logos, blurry, distorted faces, low quality, CGI",
        "camera": {
          "shot_type": "medium",
          "movement": "slow_dolly_forward",
          "angle": "eye_level",
          "speed": "normal"
        },
        "lighting": "late_afternoon_melancholic",
        "mood": "loss"
      },

      "audio": {
        "voiceover": {
          "text": "Perdi €800 al mese in appuntamenti saltati. Ogni. Singolo. Giorno.",
          "voice": "it-IT-IsabellaNeural",
          "style": "empathetic",
          "start_sec": 10,
          "words_per_second": 2.3
        },
        "music": {
          "state": "peak_then_drop",
          "character": "tension_peak",
          "volume": 0.06,
          "key": "minor"
        },
        "sfx": []
      },

      "text_overlay": {
        "elements": [
          {
            "text": "Ogni. Singolo. Giorno.",
            "appear_at_sec": 15.0,
            "disappear_at_sec": 18.0,
            "position": { "x": "center", "y_percent": 65 },
            "font": "Inter-Bold",
            "font_size_px": 56,
            "color": "#FFFFFF",
            "background": { "color": "#000000", "opacity": 0.6, "border_radius_px": 8 },
            "animation_in": { "type": "slide_up", "duration_sec": 0.3 },
            "animation_out": { "type": "fade", "duration_sec": 0.2 }
          }
        ]
      },

      "transition_out": {
        "type": "hard_cut",
        "duration_sec": 0
      }
    },

    {
      "id": "S4_solution",
      "phase": "solution",
      "start_sec": 18,
      "end_sec": 25,
      "duration_sec": 7,

      "visual": {
        "type": "screenshot",
        "source": "local",
        "file": "screenshots/02-calendario.png",
        "crop": { "x": 0, "y": 0, "w": 1080, "h": 1920 },
        "ken_burns": {
          "start_scale": 1.8,
          "end_scale": 2.0,
          "focus_point": { "x": "center", "y": 40 }
        }
      },

      "audio": {
        "voiceover": {
          "text": "Con FLUXION, Sara risponde al telefono da sola. I tuoi dati restano sul tuo computer. Zero abbonamenti.",
          "voice": "it-IT-IsabellaNeural",
          "style": "hopeful",
          "start_sec": 18,
          "words_per_second": 2.8
        },
        "music": {
          "state": "playing",
          "character": "uplifting_warm",
          "volume": 0.08,
          "key": "major"
        },
        "sfx": [
          { "type": "notification_ping", "at_sec": 19.0, "volume": 0.15, "duration_sec": 0.5 }
        ]
      },

      "text_overlay": {
        "elements": [
          {
            "text": "Sara risponde per te",
            "appear_at_sec": 18.5,
            "disappear_at_sec": 23.0,
            "position": { "x": "center", "y_percent": 20 },
            "font": "Poppins-Bold",
            "font_size_px": 56,
            "color": "#22D3EE",
            "background": { "color": "#000000", "opacity": 0.6, "border_radius_px": 8 },
            "animation_in": { "type": "fade", "duration_sec": 0.3 },
            "animation_out": { "type": "fade", "duration_sec": 0.3 }
          }
        ]
      },

      "transition_out": {
        "type": "fade_to_black",
        "duration_sec": 0.5
      }
    },

    {
      "id": "S5_cta",
      "phase": "cta",
      "start_sec": 25,
      "end_sec": 30,
      "duration_sec": 5,

      "visual": {
        "type": "generated_frame",
        "background": "#000000",
        "logo": {
          "file": "assets/logo_fluxion.jpg",
          "position": { "x": "center", "y_percent": 15 },
          "scale": 0.3
        }
      },

      "audio": {
        "voiceover": {
          "text": "FLUXION. Quattrocentonovantasette euro. Una volta. Per sempre.",
          "voice": "it-IT-IsabellaNeural",
          "style": "confident",
          "start_sec": 25,
          "words_per_second": 2.0,
          "pre_silence_sec": 0.5
        },
        "music": {
          "state": "resolving",
          "character": "confident_close",
          "volume": 0.06,
          "key": "major"
        },
        "sfx": []
      },

      "text_overlay": {
        "elements": [
          {
            "text": "FLUXION",
            "appear_at_sec": 25.3,
            "disappear_at_sec": 30.0,
            "position": { "x": "center", "y_percent": 30 },
            "font": "Inter-Bold",
            "font_size_px": 96,
            "color": "#FFFFFF",
            "animation_in": { "type": "fade", "duration_sec": 0.4 }
          },
          {
            "text": "€497 una volta. Per sempre.",
            "appear_at_sec": 26.0,
            "disappear_at_sec": 30.0,
            "position": { "x": "center", "y_percent": 45 },
            "font": "Inter-Bold",
            "font_size_px": 72,
            "color": "#FFFFFF",
            "animation_in": { "type": "fade", "duration_sec": 0.4 }
          },
          {
            "text": "Treatwell: €4.320 in 3 anni",
            "appear_at_sec": 27.0,
            "disappear_at_sec": 30.0,
            "position": { "x": "center", "y_percent": 57 },
            "font": "Inter-Regular",
            "font_size_px": 36,
            "color": "#FF4444",
            "animation_in": { "type": "fade", "duration_sec": 0.3 }
          },
          {
            "text": "fluxion-landing.pages.dev",
            "appear_at_sec": 27.5,
            "disappear_at_sec": 30.0,
            "position": { "x": "center", "y_percent": 70 },
            "font": "Inter-Bold",
            "font_size_px": 48,
            "color": "#7799FF",
            "animation_in": { "type": "fade", "duration_sec": 0.3 }
          }
        ]
      },

      "transition_out": {
        "type": "fade_to_black",
        "duration_sec": 0.8
      }
    }
  ],

  "global_audio": {
    "music_track": {
      "file": "assets/music/tension_to_uplifting.mp3",
      "base_volume": 0.08,
      "fade_in_sec": 0.5,
      "fade_out_sec": 1.0,
      "key_change_at_sec": 18
    },
    "voiceover_config": {
      "voice": "it-IT-IsabellaNeural",
      "default_style": "empathetic",
      "output_format": "mp3"
    },
    "ducking": {
      "enabled": true,
      "music_duck_to": 0.04,
      "duck_attack_sec": 0.2,
      "duck_release_sec": 0.5
    }
  },

  "export": {
    "format": "mp4",
    "codec": "h264",
    "bitrate": "8M",
    "fps": 30,
    "audio_codec": "aac",
    "audio_bitrate": "192k"
  },

  "wa_message": {
    "template": "Ciao [nome], gestisci un salone? Questo video e fatto per te.\n\n[VIDEO_LINK]\n\nFLUXION — €497 una volta, nessun abbonamento.\nTreatwell: €120/mese + commissioni.\n\nSe ti interessa: fluxion-landing.pages.dev"
  }
}
```

### Schema Field Reference

#### `meta` — Project metadata
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `verticale` | string | YES | One of 9 vertical identifiers |
| `label` | string | YES | Human-readable vertical name |
| `duration_target_sec` | number | YES | Target duration (30, 45, or 60) |
| `aspect_ratio` | string | YES | Always "9:16" for vertical |
| `resolution` | string | YES | Always "1080x1920" |
| `formula` | string | YES | Narrative formula: "PAS", "BAB", "AIDA" |

#### `emotional_arc.beats[]` — Emotional progression
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Beat identifier |
| `start` / `end` | number | Time boundaries in seconds |
| `emotion` | string | Primary emotion: empathy, stress, anxiety, hope, empowerment |
| `intensity` | number | 0.0-1.0 emotional intensity scale |

#### `scenes[].visual` — What appears on screen
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `type` | enum | "ai_clip", "screenshot", "generated_frame" |
| `source` | string | "veo3", "runway", "local" |
| `prompt` | string | AI generation prompt (if ai_clip) |
| `negative_prompt` | string | What to avoid in AI generation |
| `camera` | object | Shot type, movement, angle, speed |
| `file` | string | Local file path (if screenshot/generated) |
| `ken_burns` | object | Start/end scale + focus point (for screenshots) |

#### `scenes[].audio` — Sound design per scene
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `voiceover` | object/null | VO text, voice, style, timing. Null = silence. |
| `music` | object | State (playing/building/peak/resolving), character, volume, key |
| `sfx` | array | Sound effects with type, timing, volume, duration |

#### `scenes[].text_overlay.elements[]` — On-screen text
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `text` | string | The text to display |
| `appear_at_sec` / `disappear_at_sec` | number | Exact timing |
| `position` | object | x alignment + y_percent (from top) |
| `font` / `font_size_px` / `color` | string/number | Typography |
| `background` | object | Semi-transparent bg: color, opacity, border_radius |
| `animation_in` / `animation_out` | object | Type (fade/slide_up/slide_down) + duration |

#### `scenes[].transition_out` — How this scene ends
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `type` | enum | "hard_cut", "cross_dissolve", "fade_to_black", "zoom_in" |
| `duration_sec` | number | Transition duration (0 for hard cut) |

### Backward Compatibility with Existing Pipeline

The proposed JSON format is a **superset** of the existing `*_prompts.json` format. A migration function can extract the old format from the new:

```python
def to_legacy_prompts_json(storyboard: dict) -> dict:
    """Convert v1 storyboard to existing prompts JSON format."""
    meta = storyboard["meta"]
    scenes = storyboard["scenes"]

    veo3_requests = []
    for i, scene in enumerate(scenes):
        if scene["visual"]["type"] == "ai_clip":
            veo3_requests.append({
                "clip": i + 1,
                "prompt": scene["visual"]["prompt"],
                "aspect_ratio": meta["aspect_ratio"],
                "duration_seconds": scene["duration_sec"],
                "negative_prompt": scene["visual"].get("negative_prompt", ""),
            })

    segments = []
    for scene in scenes:
        vo = scene["audio"].get("voiceover")
        segments.append({
            "clip": scene["id"],
            "start_sec": scene["start_sec"],
            "text": vo["text"] if vo else None,
            "voice": vo["voice"] if vo else "it-IT-IsabellaNeural",
            **({"style": vo["style"]} if vo and vo.get("style") else {}),
        })

    return {
        "verticale": meta["verticale"],
        "label": meta["label"],
        "veo3_requests": veo3_requests,
        "narration_script": {
            "verticale": meta["verticale"],
            "label": meta["label"],
            "segments": segments,
        },
        "cta_frame": _extract_cta_frame(scenes),
        "wa_message_template": storyboard.get("wa_message", {}).get("template", ""),
    }
```

### What the New Format Adds vs. Existing

| Capability | Old Format | New Format |
|------------|-----------|------------|
| Veo 3 prompts | YES | YES |
| Voiceover script | YES | YES (enhanced with timing) |
| CTA frame | YES (basic) | YES (rich with animations) |
| WA message | YES | YES |
| Emotional arc | NO | YES — guides music/pacing |
| Camera instructions | NO | YES — in visual.camera |
| Music cues per scene | NO | YES — state, key, character |
| SFX triggers | NO | YES — type, timing, volume |
| Text overlay timing | NO | YES — per-element positioning |
| Transition types | NO | YES — per-scene boundary |
| Ken Burns for screenshots | NO | YES — start/end scale |
| Export settings | NO | YES — codec, bitrate, fps |
| Audio ducking config | NO | YES — automatic VO-music balance |
| Scene phase labels | NO | YES — links to PAS formula |

---

## Appendix A: Complete Vertical Storyboard Templates

### Minimum Viable Storyboard Per Vertical

Every 30-second vertical storyboard MUST have exactly 5 scenes following this template:

| Scene | Phase | Visual Type | Duration | VO |
|-------|-------|-------------|----------|-----|
| S1 | Hook | AI clip (sector-specific pain) | 3s | Silent |
| S2 | Problem | AI clip (close-up of chaos) | 7s | Silent or light |
| S3 | Agitation | AI clip (loss/empty/missed) | 8s | Pain stat + cost |
| S4 | Solution | Screenshot (hero feature) | 7s | Feature + FLUXION benefits |
| S5 | CTA | Generated frame (black bg) | 5s | Price statement |

### Vertical-Specific Hook Pain Stats

These are the tested, researched pain statistics. Use exactly these numbers:

| Vertical | Pain Stat | Source/Basis |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| Parrucchiere | €800/mese in appuntamenti saltati | Industry avg 3-4 no-shows/day @ €30 |
| Barbiere | €35 per posto vuoto | Avg barbershop service price Italy |
| Officina | 10 chiamate/giorno = 1 ora persa | Owner-reported avg, auto repair |
| Carrozzeria | 5 minuti per ogni update manuale | Multi-step communication overhead |
| Dentista | €150 per poltrona vuota | Dental chair opportunity cost/hour |
| Centro Estetico | 50% dei clienti non torna | Retention rate without digital tracking |
| Nail Artist | 45 minuti sprecati per no-show | Avg nail appointment duration |
| Palestra | 40% non rinnova senza follow-up | Gym membership renewal statistics |
| Fisioterapista | €45 per seduta saltata | Avg physiotherapy session price Italy |

---

## Appendix B: Music Track Requirements

### Required Music Segments

For the video pipeline, each music track must have these characteristics:

| Segment | Duration | Character | BPM | Key | Instruments |
|---------|----------|-----------|-----|-----|-------------|
| Tension intro | 0-18s | Building anxiety | 75-85 | Am/Dm | Piano, subtle strings |
| Key change | 18s | Transition moment | - | Am→C / Dm→F | Chord resolution |
| Uplifting | 18-28s | Warm confidence | 100-115 | C/F major | Piano, light percussion |
| Resolution | 28-30s | Confident close | 100-115 | C/F major | Sustained chord, fade |

### Licensing Requirements

- ZERO cost (non-negotiable per FLUXION guardrail)
- Royalty-free for commercial use including social media advertising
- No attribution required (or attribution satisfiable in video description)
- Sources: Pixabay Music, Free Music Archive, YouTube Audio Library

---

## Appendix C: Voiceover Generation Parameters

### Edge-TTS Configuration

| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| Voice | it-IT-IsabellaNeural | Matches Sara's identity |
| Rate | -10% (problem/CTA), 0% (solution) | Slower for emphasis, normal for information |
| Pitch | +0Hz | Natural, not artificially high |
| Volume | +0% | Normalize in post-production |

### Pronunciation Notes for Italian TTS

| Text | Correct Pronunciation | TTS Issue |
|------|----------------------|-----------|
| "€497" | "Quattrocentonovantasette euro" | Write the number in words |
| "FLUXION" | "Fluxion" (Italian stress) | Generally pronounced correctly |
| "WhatsApp" | "Wozzapp" (Italian pronunciation) | TTS handles this correctly |
| "no-show" | "no-show" | May need "noshow" without hyphen |
| "Sara" | "Sara" (Italian, stress on first syllable) | Correct by default |

---

## Summary of Recommendations

### Immediate Actions (for existing pipeline)

1. **Increase font sizes** in `CTA_FRAME_FFMPEG`: competitor line from 28px to 36px, subtitle from 32px to 40px
2. **Add text overlay to hook** (first frame): the pain stat number should appear visually, not just in voiceover later
3. **Add SFX layer**: phone ring in hook, notification ping in solution phase
4. **Ensure music key change** at the agitation-to-solution boundary (second 18 in 30s format)

### Strategic Actions (for storyboard v2)

1. **Adopt the proposed JSON storyboard format** as the source of truth for all 9 verticals
2. **Build a `storyboard_to_capcut()` converter** that translates the JSON into VectCutAPI calls
3. **Build a `storyboard_to_ffmpeg()` converter** for the non-CapCut pipeline
4. **Implement per-vertical competitor cost comparison** in CTA frames (not just Treatwell for all)
5. **Create 3 music track variants** (tension-to-uplifting) at different tempos for A/B testing

### Quality Gates

Before publishing any vertical video:
- [ ] Hook has visual + text in frame 1
- [ ] Pain stat uses a specific euro/time number
- [ ] Voiceover starts at second 8-10, not second 0
- [ ] Music shifts from minor to major at solution phase
- [ ] CTA frame visible for minimum 5 seconds
- [ ] Price "€497" appears in both voiceover AND text
- [ ] Competitor comparison included in CTA
- [ ] URL is legible at mobile viewing distance (48px+ font)
- [ ] Total duration within 28-34 seconds (or 43-47 for 45s, or 55-62 for 60s)
- [ ] No more than 2 consecutive screenshot scenes without AI clip break

---

## Sources

### High Confidence (Official docs, multiple sources agree)
- PAS formula structure and application: [BlakSheep Creative](https://blaksheepcreative.com/digital-marketing/content-marketing/copywriting/problem-agitate-solution-formula/), [PageBlock](https://pageblock.io/resources/framework/pas)
- Text overlay safe zones and font sizing: [Drive Editor](https://driveeditor.com/blog/text-overlays-in-short-videos), [Project Aeon](https://project-aeon.com/blogs/text-overlay-on-video-master-engaging-techniques)
- Hook strategies and 1.7s attention window: [Socialync](https://www.socialync.io/blog/content-hooks-stop-scrolling-2026), [OpusClip](https://www.opus.pro/blog/youtube-shorts-hook-formulas)
- Video transition types: [Descript](https://www.descript.com/blog/article/video-transitions), [CapCut](https://www.capcut.com/resource/best-film-transitions)
- Software demo video best practices: [Testimonial Hero](https://www.testimonialhero.com/blog/demo-videos), [VideoPulse](https://www.videopulse.io/blog/how-to-create-high-converting-saas-demo-videos-2025-guide)
- Italian CTA best practices: [Italiaonline](https://www.italiaonline.it/risorse/call-to-action-come-usarla-con-successo-nel-web-marketing-1006), [Inside Marketing](https://www.insidemarketing.it/glossario/definizione/call-to-action/)

### Medium Confidence (WebSearch verified with one official source)
- Music role in video ads (+9pt awareness): [Spotify Advertising](https://ads.spotify.com/en-US/best-practices-guide/creative-video-ads/)
- Voiceover-music balance: [Voice Crafters](https://www.voicecrafters.com/blog/music-for-your-marketing-video/), [Bunny Studio](https://bunnystudio.com/blog/voice-over-background-music-best-practices/)
- JSON storyboard format for AI video: [LTX Studio](https://ltx.studio/blog/json-prompting-for-video-image-generation), [JSON Prompt Studio](https://jsonpromptstudio.com/)
- CapCut project format: [Tommy McCracken](https://tommy-mccracken.com/archives/134)
- Italian PMI digital marketing behavior: [Hangler](https://www.hangler.it/en/post/web-marketing-pmi-data-from-the-italian-context)

### Low Confidence (Single source, needs validation)
- 30-second video ad completion rate benchmarks (cited as "gold standard" by Gourmet Ads)
- Specific competitor pricing for non-salon verticals (Fisiapp, YouDox, BodyShop Manager — needs verification)
- SaaS demo video 10-20% conversion range (VideoPulse claim, not independently verified)
